core_mechanism: Krein-signature analysis of a non-self-adjoint symplectic (Hamiltonian) eigenvalue problem: eigenvalues carrying a definite Krein sign are forced to be simple and confined to the unit circle, which lets the discrete spectrum be located and counted.
brief_reason: The abstract locates and counts the discrete eigenvalues of a non-self-adjoint spectral problem by exhibiting its symplectic structure and invoking Krein stability theory, showing that eigenvalues of definite Krein signature are simple and lie on the unit circle.
